deals_cleared.merger_name: National Dental Care – business assets comprising the Comfy Dental clinic, Mandurah, WA

targets.name: The Trustee for the Comfy Dental Trust

deals_cleared.merger_description: NDC HoldCo Pty Ltd (together with its subsidiaries, NDC), through its wholly-owned subsidiary, NDC Australia Pty Limited, proposes to acquire from the Comfy Dental Trust 100% of the business assets comprising the Comfy Dental Clinic (Comfy Dental).

events.title: Questionnaire - National Dental Care – business assets comprising the Comfy Dental clinic, Mandurah, WA
events.display_title: Questionnaire - National Dental Care – business assets comprising the Comfy Dental clinic, Mandurah, WA
events.url_gh: /mergers/MN-50007/Questionnaire - National Dental Care – business assets comprising the Comfy Dental clinic, Mandurah, WA.pdf

events.title: National Dental Care - business assets comprising the Comfy Dental clinic, Mandurah, WA - Phase 1 determination
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Philip Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
NDC HoldCo Pty Ltd (together with its subsidiaries, NDC), through its
wholly owned subsidiary, NDC Australia Pty Limited, proposes to
acquire from the Comfy Dental Trust 100% of the business assets
comprising the Comfy Dental Clinic (Comfy Dental) (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, NDC, is a dental services organisation that operates a
network of dental clinics that provide a range of general dentistry
services. NDC has dental clinics in all Australian states and
territories except Tasmania. It has one existing dental clinic located
in Mandurah, WA (DB Dental Mandurah). NDC is majority owned by
funds managed by Crescent Capital Partners.
The target, Comfy Dental is a dental clinic located in Mandurah, WA
that provides a range of general dentistry services.
The parties overlap in the supply of general dentistry services.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form and publicly available information.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In
reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC
makes the following findings.
• The aggregation in the supply of general dentistry services in the
local area arising from the Acquisition is not significant. This
assessment is not impacted by consideration of previous
acquisitions by NDC in the last three years.
• NDC will continue to face competition from a number of
alternative general dental clinics in the local area surrounding
the target.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.

events.url_gh: /mergers/MN-50007/Phase 1 Determination - National Dental Care – business assets comprising the Comfy Dental clinic, Mandurah, WA.pdf

deals_cleared.merger_description: MidOcean Energy proposes to indirectly acquire a:

events.title: Questionnaire - MidOcean Energy - JERA Gorgon Pty Ltd
events.display_title: Questionnaire - MidOcean Energy - JERA Gorgon Pty Ltd

events.title: MidOcean Energy - JERA Gorgon - Phase 1 determination
events.display_title: MidOcean Energy - JERA Gorgon - Phase 1 determination
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
MidOcean Energy proposes to indirectly acquire a:
• ~0.417% interest in the Gorgon LNG Project located on
Barrow Island, Western Australia, and
• ~0.735% interest in the Ichthys LNG Project located in the
Browse Basin in offshore Western Australia, including in the
exploration activities of the expired WA-285-P tenement
via the acquisition by a newly incorporated subsidiary, MidOcean
Australia Holdings 2 Pty Ltd (MidOcean Holdings) of 100% of the
shares in JERA Gorgon Pty Ltd (JERA Gorgon) from JERA Australia
Pty Ltd (JERA Australia), pursuant to a share sale agreement (the
Acquisition).
The Acquisition has given rise to a pre-empt right in favour of the
other joint venture participants to the Ichthys LNG Project. As a
result, one of the Ichthys joint venture participants has exercised its
right to acquire the underlying Ichthys LNG Project interests held
indirectly by JERA Gorgon. This subsequent acquisition would occur
following completion of the Acquisition and as such, has not been
considered as part of this determination.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

MidOcean Energy
The acquirer, MidOcean Holdings, is part of MidOcean Energy, which
is a liquefied natural gas (LNG) company that supplies natural gas in
Australia and LNG and condensate overseas. It holds minority
interests in LNG projects located in Western Australia (WA) and
Queensland, including a:
• 1% interest in the Gorgon LNG Project: a three train LNG
facility and domestic gas plant on Barrow Island, WA,
• 5% interest in the Pluto LNG Project: a one train LNG project
on the Burrup Peninsula, WA, and
• 1.25% interest in the QCLNG Project: a two-train onshore
coal seam gas project on Curtis Island, Queensland.
MidOcean Energy is managed by EIG Management Company, LLC
and its affiliates, a US institutional investor that specialises in
investments in energy and energy-related infrastructure globally.
JERA Gorgon
The target, JERA Gorgon, is a subsidiary of JERA Australia, which in
turn is owned by a Japanese-incorporated power generation
company. JERA Gorgon supplies natural gas in Australia and LNG,
liquefied petroleum gas and condensate overseas. Its interests in
Australia includes:
• a ~0.417% interest in the Gorgon LNG Project, and
• 100% of the shares in JERA Ichthys Pty Ltd, which in turn
holds ~0.735% interest in the Ichthys LNG Project and its
associated exploration permit. The Ichthys LNG Project
extracts gas in WA which is transported via pipeline to a
two-train LNG facility in the Northern Territory.

MidOcean Energy and JERA Gorgon (the Parties) horizontally
overlap in the production and supply of natural gas in WA.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form and publicly available information.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In
reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC
makes the following findings.
• The Acquisition would result in a very small change in MidOcean
Energy’s shareholding in the Gorgon LNG Project that would not
materially change MidOcean Energy’s influence or control over
the project.
• The Acquisition would provide MidOcean Energy with a very
small shareholding in the Ichthys LNG Project that would not
confer control or influence over the project.
• The Acquisition would lead to a very small increase in MidOcean
Energy’s share of gas production in WA. MidOcean Energy would
remain a very small supplier of domestic gas in WA and continue
to face competition from a range of other suppliers.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.

events.url_gh: /mergers/MN-05004/MidOcean Energy - JERA Gorgon - Phase 1 determination without conditions.pdf

deals_cleared.merger_description: CVS Vets (Australia) Pty Ltd, a wholly owned subsidiary of CVS (Australia) Holdings Pty Ltd which is part of CVS Group Plc (together, **CVS Group**) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares of Pittwater Animal Hospital, a small animal veterinary practice in Warriewood in Sydney's Northern Beaches region (the **Acquisition**).

events.title: Timeline extended by 10 business days – following request by parties
events.display_title: Timeline extended by 10 business days – following request by parties

events.title: CVS Group - Pittwater Animal Hospital - Phase 1 determination
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
CVS Vets (Australia) Pty Ltd, a wholly owned subsidiary of CVS
(Australia) Holdings Pty Ltd which is part of CVS Group Plc
(together, CVS Group) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares of
Pittwater Animal Hospital (Pittwater), a small animal veterinary
practice in Warriewood in Sydney's Northern Beaches region
(the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, CVS Group, is an integrated veterinary services
provider based in the United Kingdom and listed on the London
Stock Exchange. In Australia, CVS Group owns a number of
veterinary practices in Queensland, New South Wales, the Australian
Capital Territory, Victoria, South Australia and Western Australia.
CVS Group owns a number of veterinary practices in metropolitan
Sydney, including holding a majority interest in Sydney Animal
Hospitals (SAH). SAH operates two clinics in the Northern Beaches
region, SAH Newport and SAH Avalon.
The target, Pittwater, is a veterinary practice located in Warriewood
in Sydney’s Northern Beaches region.
The reasons for the ACCC’s determination are set out in section 2.

deals_cleared.merger_name: Coles – supermarket site in New Norfolk (TAS)
deals_cleared.merger_description: Coles Supermarkets Australia Pty Ltd, a subsidiary of Coles Group Limited (**Coles**), has signed a letter of offer with Tipalea Private No.31 Pty Ltd (ACN 686 197 115) to facilitate Coles leasing a new standalone supermarket to be developed at 120 Glebe Road, New Norfolk, Tasmania 7140 (the **Property**).

events.title: Questionnaire - Coles - supermarket site in New Norfolk (TAS)
events.display_title: Questionnaire - Coles - supermarket site in New Norfolk (TAS)

events.title: Coles New Norfolk - Phase 1 Determination
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Coles Supermarkets Australia Pty Ltd, a subsidiary of Coles Group
Limited (Coles), proposes to acquire a leasehold interest at 120
Glebe Road, New Norfolk, Tasmania 7140 (the Target Site) to
establish a new standalone supermarket site from Tipalea Private
No.31 Pty Ltd (Tipalea) (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, Coles, is an Australian public company (ASX:COL) that
primarily operates:
• supermarkets nationwide, and
• retail liquor stores nationwide, trading under the Liquorland,
Liquorland Warehouse and Liquorland Cellars brands.
The vendor, Tipalea, is an Australian private company specialising in
property development and investment in New South Wales, Victoria,
Queensland and Tasmania.
The Target Site is located in New Norfolk, Tasmania, approximately
35km northwest of Hobart. The Target Site is currently occupied by a
nursery garden and supplies shop.

The leasehold interest would be an input to Coles’ supermarket
retailing operations.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form, other information provided by the parties and
publicly available sources.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In
reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC
makes the following findings:
• Pursuant to the Acquisition, Coles proposes to open a new
supermarket site in New Norfolk. Coles does not currently
operate in New Norfolk or the surrounding areas. The closest
existing Coles supermarket at Bridgewater is 18.5km driving
distance from the Target Site.
• The Acquisition would introduce a competing grocery retailer to
the existing full line Woolworths supermarket and the smaller
IGA X-Press in New Norfolk.
• The Target Site is not currently used for grocery retailing.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.

deals_cleared.merger_description: Adobe Inc. (**Adobe**) through a newly incorporated direct wholly owned subsidiary, Fenway Merger Sub, Inc. (**Merger Sub**), proposes to acquire 100% of the share capital in Semrush Holdings, Inc. (Semrush and together with Adobe, the **Parties**). The Proposed Transaction is being implemented pursuant to a merger agreement entered into between Adobe, Merger Sub, and Semrush dated 18 November 2025 (**Merger Agreement**). Pursuant to the Merger Agreement, Merger Sub will merge with and into Semrush, such that Merger Sub will cease to exist and Semrush will become the "surviving corporation" and a direct, wholly owned subsidiary of Adobe. After the Proposed Transaction, Semrush will be solely controlled by Adobe.

events.title: Adobe - Semrush - Phase 1 Determination
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Adobe Inc. (Adobe) through a newly incorporated direct wholly
owned subsidiary, Fenway Merger Sub, Inc. (Merger Sub), proposes
to acquire 100% of the share capital in Semrush Holdings, Inc.
(Semrush) (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, Adobe, is a global technology company. Adobe's main
offerings can be divided between its Customer Experience
Orchestration business, which focuses on accelerating customer
experience and delivering business intelligence services, and its
Creativity & Productivity business, which focuses on helping users
create, design, and publish rich content and 3D experiences, and
improve how they view, share, and collaborate on documents and
content. Adobe is headquartered in California and incorporated in
Delaware, USA.
The target, Semrush, provides an online visibility management and
content marketing service offering a range of tools to manage web
brand presence, including through its search engine optimisation
toolkit. Semrush is headquartered in Massachusetts and
incorporated in Delaware, USA.

Adobe and Semrush (together, the Parties) overlap in the supply of
generative engine optimisation services in Australia. Generative
engine optimisation refers to the process of adapting a website so
that large language model platforms, such as ChatGPT, Gemini, and
Claude, can understand, extract, and cite it when answering user
queries.
Adobe supplies generative engine optimisation services through its
Large Language Model Optimiser product. Semrush supplies
generative engine optimisation services through its AI Visibility
Toolkit and Enterprise AI Optimization products.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form, information from third partes and publicly available
information.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In
reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC
makes the following findings.
• The Parties do not compete closely as the generative engine
optimisation tools they supply have different use cases.
• The Parties would continue to face competition from multiple
alternative suppliers of generative engine optimisation service
suppliers in Australia.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.

ongoing_phase_1.merger_description: Eagers Vic Pty Ltd (**Eagers**) is a subsidiary of Eagers Automotive Limited (ASX: APE), a publicly listed automotive retail group which owns and operates motor vehicle dealerships across Australia. Eagers Automotive Limited sells new and used motor vehicles through its dealerships, representing around 50 automotive brands. Eagers also provides related goods and services including motor vehicle service, parts and allied consumer finance. Eagers operates 12 dealerships in the Melbourne area, including in Toorak (Mercedes-Benz), Malvern (ULR Jaguar and ULR Land Rover) and Port Melbourne (Jaguar, Volvo, Volkswagen and Land Rover). Eagers does not currently have any Audi dealerships in Victoria.

ongoing_phase_1.merger_description: REA Financial Services Holding Co. Pty Ltd, as a subsidiary of REA Group Ltd (**REA Group**) (ASX:REA), proposes to acquire 70% of the shares in Simplicity Loans & Advisory Pty Ltd (**Simplicity Loans & Advisory**) (the **Acquisition**).

ongoing_phase_1.merger_description: Sembcorp Industries Ltd (**Sembcorp**) proposes to acquire, via share sale agreement, 100% of Pioneer Sail Holdings Pty Limited and its subsidiaries including Alinta Energy Ltd (**Alinta Energy**) and Latrobe Valley Power (Holdings) Pty Ltd (**Latrobe Valley Power**), which operates the Loy Yang B coal-fired power station (together, the **Target Group**).

events.title: On 7 April 2026, Sembcorp gave the ACCC additional information and documents in response to the ACCC’s decision under s 51ABY of the CCA. Accordingly, the effective notification date is 7 April 2026.
events.display_title: On 7 April 2026, Sembcorp gave the ACCC additional information and documents in response to the ACCC’s decision under s 51ABY of the CCA. Accordingly, the effective notification date is 7 April 2026.

events.title: In accordance with s 51ABZ of the CCA the ACCC decided that the notification filed on 11 February 2026 should be taken not to have an effective notification date because it is materially incomplete and materially misleading.
events.display_title: In accordance with s 51ABZ of the CCA the ACCC decided that the notification filed on 11 February 2026 should be taken not to have an effective notification date because it is materially incomplete and materially misleading.

ongoing_phase_1.merger_description: Eli Lilly and Company (**Eli Lilly**) proposes to acquire 100% of the issued shares in Orna Therapeutics Holdings, LLC (**Orna Therapeutics**) (the **Acquisition**).

ongoing_phase_1.merger_name: Estia Health – Norsan residential aged care business
ongoing_phase_1.merger_description: Estia Investments Pty. Ltd. (**Estia**) proposes to acquire substantially all of the business assets of three residential aged care homes from Norsan Pty Ltd (**Norsan**), being Chirnside Views Residential Aged Care, Park Lane Croydon Residential Aged Care and Willowbrae - Melton Residential Aged Care (the **Acquisition**).

ongoing_phase_1.merger_name: Swiss Re - QBE Insurance's Trade Credit and Surety business
ongoing_phase_1.merger_description: Swiss Re International SE (**Swiss Re**) proposes to acquire certain assets from QBE Insurance Holdings Pty Ltd (**QBE**) relating to its Trade Credit & Surety business (**QBE’s TC&S Business**), including data, employees and renewal rights (the **Acquisition**).

ongoing_phase_1.merger_description: Dubai Aerospace Enterprise DAE Ltd (**DAE**) proposes to acquire 100% of the issued share capital in Macquarie AirFinance Limited (**Macquarie AirFinance**) (the **Acquisition**).

ongoing_phase_1.merger_description: Lawrence & Hanson Group Pty Ltd (**Lawrence & Hanson**) proposes to acquire the business and assets of Gordon Macdonald Pty Ltd (**Gordon Macdonald**) (the **Acquisition**).

ongoing_phase_1.merger_name: Eli Lilly – acquisition of licence from CSL Behring
ongoing_phase_1.merger_description: Eli Lilly and Company (Eli Lilly) and CSL Behring LLC (CSL Behring) propose to enter into a Licence Agreement under which Eli Lilly would be granted certain exclusive rights to develop and commercialise Clazakizumab, an anti-interleukin-6 (IL-6) monoclonal antibody, for certain new indications including atrial fibrillation, atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease and other cardiovascular-related indications (the Acquisition). CSL Behring would retain exclusive rights to continue the development and commercialisation of Clazakizumab for the prevention of cardiovascular events in patients with end-stage kidney disease.

ongoing_phase_1.merger_description: Trescal Holding Pty Ltd (**Trescal**), through a wholly-owned subsidiary, proposes to acquire TR Pty Ltd’s (**TR**) calibration and equipment sales business, TechRentals Calibration & Sales (**TR Calibration**) (the **Acquisition**).

ongoing_phase_2.merger_name: Coles – supermarket and liquor site in Kalgoorlie, WA
ongoing_phase_2.merger_description: Coles Group Limited (Coles), via its subsidiary Coles Supermarkets Australia Pty Ltd, has signed a Letter of Offer to facilitate Coles acquiring a leasehold interest in a new neighbourhood centre at Lots 95-106 Great Western Highway, Somerville, Kalgoorlie (the **Property**).
